Wu Can, styledKongxiu, was fromWucheng[烏程],Wu commandery[吳郡].A
TheRecords of Wustates: When[Wu] Canwas some years old, theold lady of the lonely citymet him, and told his mother, “Thisboyhas the appearance of a chancellor.”
WhenSun Hewas serving asCounty Chief,[Wu] Canserved as a minor official.[Sun] Hesaw him as deeply extraordinary, and when[Sun] Helater became a General, he was able to nominate chief officials, so he petitioned and had[Wu] Canserve asDeputyofQu’e[曲阿丞], who was later promoted toChief Clerk. In governance, he had fame and merits. Even though he came from modest and lowly origins, he was comparable to the likes ofLu XunandBu Jingof his home commandery and was as renowned as them. WhenSun Quanwas serving asGeneral of Chariots and Cavalry, he was summoned to serve asMaster of Records, then left to serve asPrefectofShanyin[山陰令], then returned to serve asColonel Adjutant to the Military.
During the first year of theera [222], he,Lü Fan, andHe Qi, among others, all resisted theWeigeneralCao XiuatDongkou[洞口] with a navy. It happened that there was a strong breeze in the sky. The ropes of the ships snapped, causing them to drift and hit the shore. They would be captured by theWeiarmy, and some of them sunk and submerged. To the large ships that were still intact, the living people in the water all crawled up them and shouted, but the other officials feared that the ships would capsize and used dagger-axes and spears to strike them all, not receiving them.[Wu] CanandHuang Yuanwere the only ones who ordered the sailors to accept and fetch them. Their attendants believed that the ship, being too heavy, would certainly collapse.[Wu] Cansaid, “If the ship collapses, we will all die together! These people are in a desperate situation, how could we leave them behind?” Those who[Wu] Canand[Huang] Yuansaved numbered over one hundred people.
When he returned, he was promoted toGrand AdministratorofKuaiji[會稽太守]. He summoned a reclusive scholar,Xie Tan, to serve asOfficer of Merit, but[Xie] Tandid not go due to illness.[Wu] Cangave him this order, saying, “A yinglong is divine with its bends and cranes, and a fenghuang is noble with its glamorous caws. Why would they seclude themselves even beyond the heavens and hide their scales even in the deepest abyss?”
[Wu] Canrecruited and gathered many soldiers, and was conferred asHousehold General who Illuminates Justice. He andLü Daisuppressed and pacified the Shanyue. He entered to serve asCommandant of Garrison CavalryandMinister Steward, then was promoted toGrand Tutorfor theHeir Apparent.
When the struggle of the two palaces happened,1he spoke bluntly and adhered to properness, explaining the distinction between the principal and the common.2He wanted to have thePrinceofLu[魯王],[Sun] Ba, leave [the central government] to garrisonXiakou[夏口] and send awayYang Zhuso as to not let him be in the capital. He also informedLu Xunof the news on several occasions.[Lu] Xunwas garrisoned atWuchang[武昌], but he made several petitions with strident protests. Due to this, he [Wu Can] was slandered by[Sun] Baand[Yang] Zhu, among others. He was imprisoned andexecuted.•••
Refers to the princesSun HeandSun Ba, who had their own palaces.Wu CansupportedSun He.↩
Here, “the distinction between the principal and the common” [嫡庶之分] is the belief that the son born the principal wife of a ruler should be ahead of the succession compared to a son born by a concubine, even if the concubine-born son was older.↩

Wu Can, styled Kongxiu, was from Wucheng in Wu commandery.AWhen Sun He1was the prefect there, Wu Can served as a minor official, but Sun He became amazed at Wu’s ability. When Sun He became a general later and was allowed to choose his own officers,2he petitioned for Wu Can to be deputy prefect of Qu’e, and then promoted him to be the chief clerk. Wu Can’s good administration earned him a good name. Though he was of a humble background, he became equals in fame with Lu Xu and Bu Jing from his home commandery. When Sun Quan became General of the Cavalry and Chariots, he summoned Wu Can to be his secretary, and later sent him out to be Prefect of Shanling. When he returned, Wu Can was made Colonel Army-Advisor.•••
This Sun He is Sun Shao’s uncle, who was originally from the Yu family but granted the Sun surname by Sun Jian.↩
Sun He was granted the prefects of Qu’e and Dantu as fief, and was given the power to pick officers to fill the offices of those two prefectures. See Sun Shao’s biography (SGZ: Wu 6).↩
Records of Wu: When Wu Can was a few years old, an old woman from Gucheng saw him and said to his mother, “This child has the bones of one who becomes a high minister.”
In the first year of Huangwu (AD 222), Wu Can along with Lü Fan and He Qi led a navy to Dongkou to repel the attack of the Wei general Cao Xiu. They came upon a blustery day, and all the boats broke from their mooring and started drifting to the shore, where some were taken by the Wei army, and some capsized and sunk. There were a few bigger boats that were still afloat, and the men who fell into the water but who were still alive grabbed onto the sides of those boats and cried for help. The officers on the boats, though, fearing that the boats would be overloaded and sink, refused to let the men on, hitting them with their spears. Wu Can and Huang Yuan were the only ones who ordered the crew on their boats to save the overboard men. Their aides held that the boats would definitely sink because of the weight, but Wu Can said, “If the boats should sink, then we will die together. Those men are in a desperate situation, and how can I bear to abandon them?” There were more than a hundred who were saved by Wu Can and Huang Yuan.
Upon his return, he was promoted to be Grand Administrator of Kuiji. He summoned the hermit Xie Tan to work in the department of merit, but Tan declined, pleading illness. Wu Can admonished him, saying, “A dragon is considered mystical when it can bend and extend itself for its goals, and a phoenix is considered precious when it makes beautiful cries. Why must one conceal his form beyond the heavens, or hide himself in the depths of the waters?” Wu Can gathered many men to himself, and was made General of the Interior of Shining Virtues. Together with Lü Dai he pacified the Shanyue, after which he was summoned to the court to be Colonel of the Garrisoned Cavalry and Privy Treasurer. Later he was made Grand Tutor of the Crown Prince.
During the fiasco of the two princes,3Wu Can spoke out vociferously for justice be done and the rules of primogeniture upheld. He also proposed to make the Prince of Lu leave the capital to garrison at Xiakou, and to forbid Yang Zhu4from staying in the capital. He also pass the news on to Lu Xun, who was then in stationed in Wuchang; Lu Xun then wrote memorial after memorial petitioning [Sun Quan to support the crown prince]. Because of this, Sun Ba and Yang Zhu slandered against Wu Can, and Wu Can was imprisoned and thenexecuted.•••
This refers to the rivalry between Sun Ba, Prince of Lu, and Sun He, the then Crown Prince.↩
One of the chief supporters of Sun Ba.↩
